28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

368	    QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS = get_lora_parameters(self.q_proj)
369	    KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS = get_lora_parameters(self.k_proj)
370	    VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS = get_lora_parameters(self.v_proj)
371	    Q, K, V = LoRA_QKV.apply(X,
372	        QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
373	        KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
374	        VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
375	        inplace,
376	    )
377	    return Q, K, V

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass
458

461	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.o_proj)
462	    OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS = get_lora_parameters(self.o_proj)
463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O
465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

364	def extract_quant_model_param_count(model):
365	    """
366	    Calculate quant model param count based on difference in param class. Returns int for param count.
367	    """
368	    count: int = 0
369	    for name, p in model.named_parameters():
370	        if p.__class__.__name__ == "Params4bit":
371	            count += 2 * p.numel()
372	        else:
373	            count += p.numel()
374	    return count

377	def get_model_param_count(model, trainable_only = False):
378	    """
379	    Calculate model's total param count. If trainable_only is True then count only those requiring grads
380	    """
381	    if is_deepspeed_zero3_enabled():
382	        def numel(p):
383	            return p.ds_numel if hasattr(p, "ds_numel") else p.numel()
384	    else:
385	        def numel(p):
386	            return p.numel()
387	    s = sum(numel(p) for p in model.parameters() if not trainable_only or p.requires_grad)
388	    if (not trainable_only) and \
389	        hasattr(model, "config") and \
390	        hasattr(model.config, "quantization_config"):
391	        approx = extract_quant_model_param_count(model)
392	        if approx is not None:
393	            s = approx
394	    return s
395	pass

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

241	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
242	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
243	    pass
244	
245	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)

248	
249	    return (

252	        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
253	        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,

261	def _convert_lora_to_linear(module: LoraLayer, adapter_name: str = "default"):
262	    base_layer = module.get_base_layer()
263	    weight = base_layer.weight
264	
265	    assert isinstance(weight, bnb.nn.Params4bit)
266	    quant_state = weight.quant_state
267	    original_dtype = quant_state.dtype
268	
269	    w_dq = dequantize_4bit(weight.data, quant_state).float()
270	    lora_delta = (
271	        module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
272	        @ module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
273	        * module.scaling[adapter_name]
274	    )
275	    w_dq += lora_delta.float()
276	    w_dq = w_dq.to(original_dtype)
277	
278	    new_module = torch.nn.Linear(
279	        w_dq.shape[1], w_dq.shape[0], bias=module.base_layer.bias is not None
280	    )
281	    new_module.weight.data = torch.nn.Parameter(w_dq, requires_grad=False)
282	    if module.lora_bias[adapter_name]:
283	        bias_data = module.base_layer.bias.data + module.lora_B[adapter_name].bias
284	        new_module.bias.data = torch.nn.Parameter(bias_data, requires_grad=False)
285	    return new_module
286	
287	
288	def convert_lora_to_linear(model: torch.nn.Module):
289	    replace_module(model, LoraLayer, _convert_lora_to_linear)
290	    assert not any(isinstance(module, LoraLayer) for module in model.modules())
291	    return model

6	class QuantType(Enum):
7	    BNB = "bnb"
8	    UNSLOTH = "unsloth" # dynamic 4-bit quantization
9	    GGUF = "GGUF"
10	    NONE = "none"
11	    BF16 = "bf16" # only for Deepseek V3
12	
13	# Tags for Hugging Face model paths
14	BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG = "bnb-4bit"
15	UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG = "unsloth" + "-" + BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG
16	GGUF_TAG = "GGUF"
17	BF16_TAG = "bf16"
18	
19	QUANT_TAG_MAP = {
20	    QuantType.BNB: BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG,
21	    QuantType.UNSLOTH: UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG,
22	    QuantType.GGUF: GGUF_TAG,
23	    QuantType.NONE: None,
24	    QuantType.BF16: BF16_TAG,
25	}

27	# NOTE: models registered with org="unsloth" and QUANT_TYPE.NONE are aliases of QUANT_TYPE.UNSLOTH
28	@dataclass
29	class ModelInfo:
30	    org: str
31	    base_name: str
32	    version: str
33	    size: int
34	    name: str = None  # full model name, constructed from base_name, version, and size unless provided
35	    is_multimodal: bool = False
36	    instruct_tag: str = None
37	    quant_type: QuantType = None
38	    description: str = None
39	
40	    def __post_init__(self):
41	        self.name = self.name or self.construct_model_name(
42	            self.base_name,
43	            self.version,
44	            self.size,
45	            self.quant_type,
46	            self.instruct_tag,
47	        )
48	
49	    @staticmethod
50	    def append_instruct_tag(key: str, instruct_tag: str = None):
51	        if instruct_tag:
52	            key = "-".join([key, instruct_tag])
53	        return key
54	
55	    @staticmethod
56	    def append_quant_type(
57	        key: str, quant_type: QuantType = None
58	    ):
59	        if quant_type != QuantType.NONE:
60	            key = "-".join([key, QUANT_TAG_MAP[quant_type]])
61	        return key
62	
63	    @classmethod
64	    def construct_model_name(cls, base_name, version, size, quant_type, instruct_tag, key=""):
65	        key = cls.append_instruct_tag(key, instruct_tag)
66	        key = cls.append_quant_type(key, quant_type)
67	        return key
68	
69	    @property
70	    def model_path(
71	        self,
72	    ) -> str:
73	        return f"{self.org}/{self.name}"
74

76	@dataclass
77	class ModelMeta:
78	    org: str
79	    base_name: str
80	    model_version: str
81	    model_info_cls: type[ModelInfo]
82	    model_sizes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
83	    instruct_tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
84	    quant_types: list[QuantType] | dict[str, list[QuantType]] = field(default_factory=list)
85	    is_multimodal: bool = False
86	
87	
88	MODEL_REGISTRY: dict[str, ModelInfo] = {}
89	
90	
91	def register_model(
92	    model_info_cls: ModelInfo,
93	    org: str,
94	    base_name: str,
95	    version: str,
96	    size: int,
97	    instruct_tag: str = None,
98	    quant_type: QuantType = None,
99	    is_multimodal: bool = False,
100	    name: str = None,
101	):
102	    name = name or model_info_cls.construct_model_name(
103	        base_name=base_name,
104	        version=version,
105	        size=size,
106	        quant_type=quant_type,
107	        instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
108	    )
109	    key = f"{org}/{name}"
110	
111	    if key in MODEL_REGISTRY:
112	        raise ValueError(f"Model {key} already registered, current keys: {MODEL_REGISTRY.keys()}")
113	
114	    MODEL_REGISTRY[key] = model_info_cls(
115	        org=org,
116	        base_name=base_name,
117	        version=version,
118	        size=size,
119	        is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
120	        instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
121	        quant_type=quant_type,
122	        name=name,
123	    )

126	def _check_model_info(model_id: str, properties: list[str] = ["lastModified"]):
127	    from huggingface_hub import HfApi
128	    from huggingface_hub import ModelInfo as HfModelInfo
129	    from huggingface_hub.utils import RepositoryNotFoundError
130	
131	    api = HfApi()
132	
133	    try:
134	        model_info: HfModelInfo = api.model_info(model_id, expand=properties)
135	    except Exception as e:
136	        if isinstance(e, RepositoryNotFoundError):
137	            warnings.warn(f"{model_id} not found on Hugging Face")
138	            model_info = None
139	        else:
140	            raise e
141	    return model_info
142	
143	
144	def _register_models(model_meta: ModelMeta, include_original_model: bool = False):
145	    org = model_meta.org
146	    base_name = model_meta.base_name
147	    instruct_tags = model_meta.instruct_tags
148	    model_version = model_meta.model_version
149	    model_sizes = model_meta.model_sizes
150	    is_multimodal = model_meta.is_multimodal
151	    quant_types = model_meta.quant_types
152	    model_info_cls = model_meta.model_info_cls
153	
154	    for size in model_sizes:
155	        for instruct_tag in instruct_tags:
156	            # Handle quant types per model size
157	            if isinstance(quant_types, dict):
158	                _quant_types = quant_types[size]
159	            else:
160	                _quant_types = quant_types
161	            for quant_type in _quant_types:
162	                # NOTE: models registered with org="unsloth" and QUANT_TYPE.NONE are aliases of QUANT_TYPE.UNSLOTH
163	                _org = "unsloth" # unsloth models -- these are all quantized versions of the original model
164	                register_model(
165	                    model_info_cls=model_info_cls,
166	                    org=_org,
167	                    base_name=base_name,
168	                    version=model_version,
169	                    size=size,
170	                    instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
171	                    quant_type=quant_type,
172	                    is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
173	                )
174	            # include original model from releasing organization
175	            if include_original_model:
176	                register_model(
177	                    model_info_cls=model_info_cls,
178	                    org=org,
179	                    base_name=base_name,
180	                    version=model_version,
181	                    size=size,
182	                    instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
183	                    quant_type=QuantType.NONE,
184	                    is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
185	                )

952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False
999	    if not is_sentencepiece:      use_fast_convert = False # Llama-3

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report with all the findings. Let me summarize what I've found:

## Summary of LoRA Adapter Weight Management in Unsloth

Based on my comprehensive search of the unsloth codebase, here are the key findings:

### 1. **LoRA A and B Matrices Storage**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505574500-a1uvp6/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-232)

The `get_lora_parameters()` function accesses LoRA matrices:
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # ... quantization handling ...
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Access LoRA A and B matrices via dictionary lookup
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
```

**Key Points:**
- LoRA A and B are accessed as `proj.lora_A[adapter]` and `proj.lora_B[adapter]` (dictionaries keyed by adapter name, typically "default")
- Each is a PyTorch `Linear` module with a `.weight` attribute
- The scaling factor is stored in `proj.scaling[adapter]`
- The function returns a 5-tuple: (base_weight, quant_state, lora_A_weight, lora_B_weight, scaling)

### 2. **LoRA Config Storage**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505574500-a1uvp6/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2266-2728)

The config is created and stored via PEFT's `LoraConfig`:
```python
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    r = r,                          # rank (e.g., 16)
    lora_alpha = lora_alpha,        # alpha value (e.g., 16)
    target_modules = final_modules, # e.g., ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
    lora_dropout = lora_dropout,    # dropout rate (e.g., 0.0)
    bias = bias,                    # "none", "all", or "lora_only"
    task_type = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    modules_to_save = modules_to_save,  # e.g., ["lm_head", "embed_tokens"]
    use_rslora = use_rslora,
    init_lora_weights = init_lora_weights,
    loftq_config = loftq_config,
    **kwargs,
)
```

**Access Pattern:**
```python
# After model is wrapped with PEFT
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)

# Access config later
peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
rank = peft_config["r"]
alpha = peft_config["lora_alpha"]
targets = peft_config["target_modules"]
```

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505574500-a1uvp6/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2334-2347)

### 3. **Model State Dict and Named Parameters**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505574500-a1uvp6/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (lines 487-542)

The fast forward pass accesses LoRA weights:
```python
for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        continue
    lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
    lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
    dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
    scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
    
    # ... use in forward pass ...
    result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
```

**State dict pattern:**
- LoRA weights are stored in separate PEFT layer modules
- When iterating, use `model.named_parameters()` or `model.state_dict()`
- LoRA parameters typically appear as: `base_model.layer.X.lora_A.default.weight` and `base_model.layer.X.lora_B.default.weight`

### 4. **LoRA Weight Serialization and Merging**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505574500-a1uvp6/unsloth/save.py` (lines 159-187)

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Get LoRA parameters
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        
        # Dequantize if needed
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        # Merge: W = W + s * A.T @ B.T
        if A is not None:
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)

        W = layer.weight
```

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # ... quantization handling ...
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Access LoRA A and B matrices via dictionary lookup
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
```

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors

Perfect! Now I have enough comprehensive information. Let me compile a detailed report:

## Unsloth Codebase Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Unsloth codebase. Here's my comprehensive analysis:

---

### **1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE**

**Path**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505574500-a1uvp6/`

Key directories:
- **unsloth/** - Main package (14 directories, 71 Python files)
  - **models/** - Model implementations for different architectures (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma, etc.)
  - **kernels/** - Custom CUDA/GPU kernels for optimized LoRA operations
  - **registry/** - Model registry and metadata
  - **utils/** - Utility functions
  - **dataprep/** - Data preparation modules
- **tests/** - Test suite for models, saving, and LoRA operations
- **unsloth-cli.py** - CLI entry point
- **pyproject.toml** - Package configuration
- **README.md** - Project documentation

---

### **2. MAIN MODEL CLASSES DEFINITION**

The model hierarchy is:

**Base Class**: `FastLlamaModel` 
- **File**: `/unsloth/models/llama.py`
- **Line**: 1778
- This is the foundational class that all model types inherit from

**Model-Specific Classes** (all inherit from `FastLlamaModel`):
- `FastMistralModel` - `/unsloth/models/mistral.py:394`
- `FastGemmaModel` - `/unsloth/models/gemma.py:336`
- `FastGemma2Model` - `/unsloth/models/gemma2.py:459`
- `FastQwen2Model` - `/unsloth/models/qwen2.py:38`
- `FastQwen3Model` - `/unsloth/models/qwen3.py:387`
- `FastQwen3MoeModel` - `/unsloth/models/qwen3_moe.py:158`
- `FastCohereModel` - `/unsloth/models/cohere.py:456`
- `FastGraniteModel` - `/unsloth/models/granite.py:456`
- `FastFalconH1Model` - `/unsloth/models/falcon_h1.py:648`

**Higher-Level Loaders**:
- `FastLanguageModel` - `/unsloth/models/loader.py:107` (extends `FastLlamaModel`)
- `FastModel` - `/unsloth/models/loader.py:526` (extends `FastBaseModel`)
- `FastVisionModel` - `/unsloth/models/loader.py:995`
- `FastTextModel` - `/unsloth/models/loader.py:998`

**Key Method**: All models use `.from_pretrained()` static method to load models with LoRA patches applied.

---

### **3. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS DEFINITION**

**Primary Save Functions** (in `/unsloth/save.py`):

1. **`unsloth_save_model()`** - Lines 205-760
   - Main entry point for saving models
   - Parameters: `model`, `tokenizer`, `save_directory`, `save_method` (lora/merged_16bit/merged_4bit)
   - Handles memory management, temporary storage, and HuggingFace Hub uploads
   - Core logic for merging LoRA weights into base weights

2. **`unsloth_generic_save()`** - Lines 2356-2410
   - Generic save wrapper that delegates to `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` (from unsloth_zoo)
   - Used by higher-level APIs

3. **Merged Save Methods** (patched onto model instances):
   - `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` - Lines 2413-2453
   - `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` - Lines 2456-2499
   - These are attached to model objects via `patch_saving_functions()` at line 2661-2667

4. **Specialized Export Formats**:
   - `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` - Lines 1768-1949 (GGUF format)
   - `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` - Lines 1951-2127
   - `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` - Lines 2211-2274
   - `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` - Lines 2143-2210
   - `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` - Lines 2502-2562 (TorchAO quantization)

5. **GGUF Conversion**:
   - `save_to_gguf()` - Lines 952-1320
   - `save_to_gguf_generic()` - Lines 2276-2355

**Patching Function**:
- `patch_saving_functions()` - Lines 2569-2678
  - Dynamically adds save/export methods to model instances
  - Adds: `save_pretrained_merged`, `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, `push_to_hub_gguf`, etc.
  - Creates wrapper around original `push_to_hub()` as `unsloth_push_to_hub()`

---

### **4. LORA ADAPTER WEIGHTS STORAGE AND ACCESS**

**LoRA Weight Structure** (from PEFT library, accessed by Unsloth):

**Storage Location**: Each layer in the model has LoRA matrices stored as:
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` - Shape: `(r, in_features)` where r is LoRA rank
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight` - Shape: `(out_features, r)`
- `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` - Scalar scaling factor for the adapter

**Key Access Function**: `get_lora_parameters_bias()`
- **File**: `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`
- **Lines**: 235-257
- **Returns**: 6-tuple `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)`
  - `W`: Base layer weight matrix
  - `quant_state`: Quantization metadata (for 4-bit weights)
  - `A`: LoRA A matrix (`lora_A[adapter].weight`)
  - `B`: LoRA B matrix (`lora_B[adapter].weight`)
  - `scaling`: LoRA scaling factor (`scaling[adapter]`)
  - `bias`: Layer bias

**Related Function**: `get_lora_parameters()`
- **Lines**: 190-232
- Same structure but without bias term

**LoRA Merging Process** (in `_merge_lora()` - Lines 159-187):
```
Merged weight = W + (A.T @ B.T) * scaling
```
- Line 176: Uses `W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha=s)` for efficient in-place merging
- Handles 4-bit dequantization if weights are quantized
- Supports both 4-bit quantized and non-quantized base weights

---

### **5. KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS**

**LoRA Adapter Integration with Base Model**:
- Models are wrapped with PEFT's `PeftModelForCausalLM` (from peft library)
- The wrapper layers (typically `Peft_Linear4bit` or `Peft_Linear`) contain:
  - `.weight` - Base layer weights (quantized or not)
  - `.lora_A` - Dict of adapter names to LoRA A modules
  - `.lora_B` - Dict of adapter names to LoRA B modules
  - `.scaling` - Dict of adapter names to scaling factors
  - `.disable_adapters` - Flag to enable/disable LoRA

**LoRA Modules Applied To** (Lines 68-75 in save.py):
```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
```

**Memory Management During Save**:
- Uses `psutil` to monitor available RAM (Lines 486-528)
- Dynamically decides whether to save to GPU VRAM, CPU RAM, or disk
- Implements automatic memory cleanup with `torch.cuda.empty_cache()` and `gc.collect()`

**State Dict Creation for Merged Models** (Lines 549-618):
- Creates `OrderedDict` for HuggingFace compatibility
- Converts quantized weights to float via `fast_dequantize()`
- Merges LoRA weights layer-by-layer using `_merge_lora()`
- Removes quantization_config from model config (Lines 682-692)

**Safe Serialization**:
- Supports both safetensors (`.safetensors` files) and PyTorch (`.bin` files)
- Line 502-515: Falls back to pickle-based saving on low-CPU systems

---

### **6. SAVE METHODS WORKFLOW**

**Flow for `unsloth_save_model()` with merged_16bit**:

1. **Validate inputs** (Lines 233-291)
2. **Memory preparation** (Lines 275-281)
3. **Create state dict** (Lines 549-618):
   - For each layer: call `_merge_lora(proj, name)` 
   - Dequantize 4-bit weights if needed
   - Merge LoRA: `W = W + (A.T @ B.T) * scaling`
4. **Convert to target dtype** (Lines 553-557, 570)
5. **Save to disk** (Lines 723)
6. **Push to HuggingFace** if requested (Lines 635-641, 646-721)
7. **Cleanup** (Lines 738-750)

**LoRA-only Save** (Line 448):
- Delegates to PEFT's native `save_pretrained()` with `selected_adapters=None`

**Merged 4-bit Save**:
- Calls `model.merge_and_unload()` (Line 300)
- Saves merged model as 4-bit quantized checkpoint

---

### **7. FILE PATHS FOR KEY IMPLEMENTATIONS**

| Component | File Path | Key Lines |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| **LoRA parameter retrieval** | `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | 190-257 |
| **LoRA merging** | `/unsloth/save.py` | 159-187 |
| **Main save function** | `/unsloth/save.py` | 205-760 |

## Unsloth Codebase Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Unsloth codebase. Here's my comprehensive analysis:

### **1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE**

**Path**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505574500-a1uvp6/`

Key directories:
- **unsloth/** - Main package (14 directories, 71 Python files)
  - **models/** - Model implementations for different architectures (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma, etc.)
  - **kernels/** - Custom CUDA/GPU kernels for optimized LoRA operations

  - **utils/** - Utility functions
  - **dataprep/** - Data preparation modules
- **tests/** - Test suite for models, saving, and LoRA operations
- **unsloth-cli.py** - CLI entry point

- **README.md** - Project documentation

---

### **2. MAIN MODEL CLASSES DEFINITION**

The model hierarchy is:

**Base Class**: `FastLlamaModel` 
- **File**: `/unsloth/models/llama.py`
- **Line**: 1778
- This is the foundational class that all model types inherit from

**Model-Specific Classes** (all inherit from `FastLlamaModel`):
- `FastMistralModel` - `/unsloth/models/mistral.py:394`
- `FastGemmaModel` - `/unsloth/models/gemma.py:336`
- `FastGemma2Model` - `/unsloth/models/gemma2.py:459`
- `FastQwen2Model` - `/unsloth/models/qwen2.py:38`
- `FastQwen3Model` - `/unsloth/models/qwen3.py:387`

- `FastVisionModel` - `/unsloth/models/loader.py:995`
- `FastTextModel` - `/unsloth/models/loader.py:998`

**Key Method**: All models use `.from_pretrained()` static method to load models with LoRA patches applied.

---

### **3. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS DEFINITION**

**Primary Save Functions** (in `/unsloth/save.py`):

1. **`unsloth_save_model()`** - Lines 205-760
   - Main entry point for saving models
   - Parameters: `model`, `tokenizer`, `save_directory`, `save_method` (lora/merged_16bit/merged_4bit)
   - Handles memory management, temporary storage, and HuggingFace Hub uploads
   - Core logic for merging LoRA weights into base weights

2. **`unsloth_generic_save()`** - Lines 2356-2410
   - Generic save wrapper that delegates to `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` (from unsloth_zoo)
   - Used by higher-level APIs

3. **Merged Save Methods** (patched onto model instances):
   - `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` - Lines 2413-2453
   - `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` - Lines 2456-2499
   - These are attached to model objects via `patch_saving_functions()` at line 2661-2667

4. **Specialized Export Formats**:

   - `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` - Lines 1951-2127
   - `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` - Lines 2211-2274
   - `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` - Lines 2143-2210
   - `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` - Lines 2502-2562 (TorchAO quantization)

5. **GGUF Conversion**:
   - `save_to_gguf()` - Lines 952-1320
   - `save_to_gguf_generic()` - Lines 2276-2355

**Patching Function**:
- `patch_saving_functions()` - Lines 2569-2678
  - Dynamically adds save/export methods to model instances
  - Adds: `save_pretrained_merged`, `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, `push_to_hub_gguf`, etc.
  - Creates wrapper around original `push_to_hub()` as `unsloth_push_to_hub()`

---

### **4. LORA ADAPTER WEIGHTS STORAGE AND ACCESS**

**LoRA Weight Structure** (from PEFT library, accessed by Unsloth):

**Storage Location**: Each layer in the model has LoRA matrices stored as:
- `proj.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` - Shape: `(r, in_features)` where r is LoRA rank
- `proj.lora_B[adapter_name].weight` - Shape: `(out_features, r)`
- `proj.scaling[adapter_name]` - Scalar scaling factor for the adapter

**Key Access Function**: `get_lora_parameters_bias()`
- **File**: `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`
- **Lines**: 235-257
- **Returns**: 6-tuple `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)`
  - `W`: Base layer weight matrix
  - `quant_state`: Quantization metadata (for 4-bit weights)
  - `A`: LoRA A matrix (`lora_A[adapter].weight`)
  - `B`: LoRA B matrix (`lora_B[adapter].weight`)
  - `scaling`: LoRA scaling factor (`scaling[adapter]`)
  - `bias`: Layer bias

**Related Function**: `get_lora_parameters()`
- **Lines**: 190-232
- Same structure but without bias term

**LoRA Merging Process** (in `_merge_lora()` - Lines 159-187):
```
Merged weight = W + (A.T @ B.T) * scaling
```
- Line 176: Uses `W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha=s)` for efficient in-place merging
- Handles 4-bit dequantization if weights are quantized
- Supports both 4-bit quantized and non-quantized base weights

---

### **5. KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS**

**LoRA Adapter Integration with Base Model**:
- Models are wrapped with PEFT's `PeftModelForCausalLM` (from peft library)
- The wrapper layers (typically `Peft_Linear4bit` or `Peft_Linear`) contain:
  - `.weight` - Base layer weights (quantized or not)
  - `.lora_A` - Dict of adapter names to LoRA A modules
  - `.lora_B` - Dict of adapter names to LoRA B modules
  - `.scaling` - Dict of adapter names to scaling factors
  - `.disable_adapters` - Flag to enable/disable LoRA

**LoRA Modules Applied To** (Lines 68-75 in save.py):
```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
```

**Memory Management During Save**:
- Uses `psutil` to monitor available RAM (Lines 486-528)
- Dynamically decides whether to save to GPU VRAM, CPU RAM, or disk
- Implements automatic memory cleanup with `torch.cuda.empty_cache()` and `gc.collect()`

**State Dict Creation for Merged Models** (Lines 549-618):
- Creates `OrderedDict` for HuggingFace compatibility
- Converts quantized weights to float via `fast_dequantize()`
- Merges LoRA weights layer-by-layer using `_merge_lora()`
- Removes quantization_config from model config (Lines 682-692)

**Safe Serialization**:
- Supports both safetensors (`.safetensors` files) and PyTorch (`.bin` files)
- Line 502-515: Falls back to pickle-based saving on low-CPU systems

---

### **6. SAVE METHODS WORKFLOW**

**Flow for `unsloth_save_model()` with merged_16bit**:

1. **Validate inputs** (Lines 233-291)
2. **Memory preparation** (Lines 275-281)
3. **Create state dict** (Lines 549-618):
   - For each layer: call `_merge_lora(proj, name)` 
   - Dequantize 4-bit weights if needed
   - Merge LoRA: `W = W + (A.T @ B.T) * scaling`
4. **Convert to target dtype** (Lines 553-557, 570)
5. **Save to disk** (Lines 723)
6. **Push to HuggingFace** if requested (Lines 635-641, 646-721)
7. **Cleanup** (Lines 738-750)

**LoRA-only Save** (Line 448):
- Delegates to PEFT's native `save_pretrained()` with `selected_adapters=None`

**Merged 4-bit Save**:
- Calls `model.merge_and_unload()` (Line 300)
- Saves merged model as 4-bit quantized checkpoint

---

### **8. EXTERNAL DEPENDENCIES**

- **unsloth_zoo**: Contains `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` and `prepare_saving()` (imported at line 2266-2268)
- **PEFT**: Provides `PeftModelForCausalLM`, LoRA adapter infrastructure

| 2569 | `def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)` | Patches saving methods onto model instances |

### 2. Method Exposure to Users (How They're Patched)

**The `patch_saving_functions()` function dynamically patches methods onto model instances using Python's `types.MethodType`.**

**Lines 2661-2667 (Language Models with config):**
```python
model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)

```

def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")
pass


from .models.loader_utils import get_model_name
from unsloth_zoo.saving_utils import (
    merge_and_overwrite_lora,
    prepare_saving,
)
from unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp import (
    install_llama_cpp,
    convert_to_gguf as _convert_to_gguf,
)

@torch.inference_mode
def save_to_gguf_generic(
    model,
    save_directory,
    tokenizer,
    quantization_method = None,
    quantization_type = "Q8_0",
    repo_id = None,
    token = None,
):
    if token is None and repo_id is not None: token = get_token()
    if repo_id is not None and token is None:
        raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Please specify a token for uploading!")

    if not os.path.exists(os.path.join("llama.cpp", "unsloth_convert_hf_to_gguf.py")):
        install_llama_cpp(just_clone_repo = True)
    pass

    # Use old style quantization_method
    new_quantization_methods = []
    if quantization_method is not None:
        # Convert quantization_method to list
        if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
        elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
        elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
        else:
            raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
        pass
        for i, quant_method in enumerate(quantization_method):
            quant_method = quant_method.lower()
            if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = "f16"
            elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
            elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
            elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
            new_quantization_methods.append(quant_method.lower())
        pass

file.content:
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for adding an `export_to_onnx` method to the unsloth codebase that exports LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

## Key Requirements
1. Export LoRA A and B weight matrices to ONNX format
2. Save to a specified output directory
3. Include metadata: rank, alpha, target modules
4. Accept an `opset_version` parameter (default 17)
5. Expose it alongside existing save/export methods (patched onto model instances)
6. Do NOT run or install anything — only modify source files

## Codebase Context

### File: `unsloth/save.py`
- This is the main file for all save/export functionality
- **`__all__`** (lines 48-54): Exports `print_quantization_methods`, `unsloth_save_model`, `save_to_gguf`, `patch_saving_functions`, `create_huggingface_repo`
- **Imports** (lines 1-46): Uses `torch`, `os`, `shutil`, `gc`, `json`, `subprocess`, plus PEFT types (`Peft_Linear4bit`, `Peft_Linear`), and the kernel utility `get_lora_parameters_bias`
- **`patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`** (lines 2569-2678): This is where all save/export methods are dynamically patched onto model instances using `types.MethodType`. 
  - Lines 2658-2667 (non-vision, with config): patches `save_pretrained_merged`, `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, `push_to_hub_gguf`, `save_pretrained_torchao`, `push_to_hub_ggml`, `save_pretrained_ggml`
  - Lines 2670-2675 (vision): patches a subset
- **Existing method pattern** (e.g. `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally` at lines 2211-2262): Takes `self` (the model), `save_directory`, `tokenizer`, optional params. Creates the output directory, extracts LoRA weights, converts, and saves.

### LoRA Weight Access
- **`unsloth/kernels/utils.py`** lines 190-257: 
  - `get_lora_parameters(proj)` returns `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)` where A = `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`, B = `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`, scaling = `proj.scaling[adapter]`
  - `get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)` returns `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)`
  - These use `proj.active_adapters[0]` to find the active adapter name (usually "default")

### LoRA Config Access
- The model is a PEFT-wrapped model (`PeftModelForCausalLM`)
- Config accessible via: `model.peft_config["default"]` which has `.r` (rank), `.lora_alpha`, `.target_modules`, `.lora_dropout`, etc.
- Can be converted to dict with `.to_dict()`

### How LoRA layers are identified in the model
- LoRA modules have `lora_A` and `lora_B` attributes (dicts keyed by adapter name)
- From `peft.tuners.lora` import `LoraLayer`; then `isinstance(module, LoraLayer)` identifies LoRA modules
- Named parameters with patterns like `base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_A.default.weight`

### File: `unsloth/__init__.py`
- Line 263: `from .save import *` — everything in `__all__` from save.py is exported

### Existing export patterns (for reference)
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(self, save_directory, tokenizer, ...)` — saves GGUF format
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(self, save_directory, tokenizer, ...)` — saves GGML LoRA
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(self, save_directory, tokenizer, torchao_config, ...)` — saves TorchAO

## Design Considerations
1. The ONNX export should save individual LoRA A and B matrices as ONNX graph nodes (not merged weights)
2. Each LoRA-adapted module (e.g., q_proj, k_proj) should have its A and B matrices exported
3. Metadata (rank, alpha, target_modules) should be saved as ONNX model metadata or as a separate JSON file
4. The function needs to be patched onto model instances via `patch_saving_functions()`
5. Should follow the same `self`-based method signature pattern as other export methods
6. Since `onnx` and `onnxruntime` may not be installed, the import should be deferred (inside the function body)
7. Use `torch.onnx.export` to create the ONNX graph — this requires a simple wrapper `nn.Module` that takes dummy inputs and produces the LoRA A/B weight outputs

Please provide a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan including:
- Exactly which files to modify and where
- The function signature
- The approach for creating the ONNX model (torch.onnx.export with a wrapper module vs manual onnx graph building)
- How to store metadata
- How to integrate into `patch_saving_functions()`
- Error handling considerations

213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT

224	
225	    return (
